subject: [PATCH v5 5/5] sunrpc: derive the pool count instead of caching it in sv_nrpools

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that increasing the interface surface between sunrpc.ko and nfsd.ko has an abstract value, but may have a concrete cost in terms of code size, and requested further evidence before objecting to the original patch
reviews.raw_body:
In general, yes.  But it is not without cost.  Sometimes the cost
matters.
Interface size has an abstract value.  Code size can have concrete
value.  Comparing them is subjective I guess.
I have no concrete evidence that there is a problem, so I'll stop
objecting.

Thanks,
NeilBrown

2026-07-09.patch_summary: This patch modifies the sunrpc code to derive the pool count dynamically instead of caching it in sv_nrpools, reducing the interface complexity between sunrpc and nfsd modules.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that NeilBrown's concern about increasing interface 'surface' between sunrpc.ko and nfsd.ko is valid, but expressed inability to revisit the idea due to lack of time.
reviews.raw_body:
Your point is certainly valid, and this _is_ a hot path. If we can
demonstrate that this matters, then I'd be happy to revisit the idea. I
don't have the cycles to do so at the moment though.

-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-07-08.patch_summary: This patch modifies the sunrpc code to derive the pool count dynamically instead of caching it in sv_nrpools, reducing the interface complexity between sunrpc and nfsd modules.
